nodes.title: Combinatorial Graph Theory
nodes.description: Paths and trees, connectivity, Eulerian and Hamiltonian cycles, matchings, edge and vertex colorings, planar graphs, Euler's formula and the Four Color Theorem, networks. Prerequisite: Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student or Instructor permission; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Numbers for Naturalists
nodes.description: Data, statistics, modeling, algebra, word problems, and calculus for students in the Rubenstein School of Environment and Natural Resources. Students who do well in the algebra section may continue with MATH 1212 or MATH 1234. Credit not awarded to College of Engineering and Mathematical Sciences students. Prerequisite: Three years of high school math.
nodes.other_requirements: Three years of high school math. RSENR students only

nodes.title: Discrete Structures
nodes.description: Introduction to analytic and formal methods of computer science with practical examples, including analysis or data structures, recursion relations, proof methods, and logic programming. Credit not awarded for both CS 1640 and MATH 2055. Prerequisites: CS 1210 or CS 2100; MATH 1234 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereqs enforced by the system: (CS 1210or2100) and (MATH 1234or1242); Open to Degree and PACE students. Credit not awarded for both CS 1640 and MATH 2055.

nodes.title: Ordinary Diffrntl Equation
nodes.description: Solutions of linear ordinary differential equations, the Laplace transformation, and series solutions of differential equations. Prerequisite: MATH 2248. Corequisite: MATH 2522 or MATH 2544. Credit not granted for more than one of the courses MATH 3230 or MATH 3201.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not granted for more than one of the courses MATH 3230 or MATH 3201. Students should have knowledge of calculus I and II (differential and integral calculus), and some sections of calculus III, before taking this class. Knowledge of linear algebra is also needed, but linear algebra can be a co-requisite. The solid knowledge of the material listed in the document at https://tlakoba.w3.uvm.edu/26_spring/math_3230/background.pdf is required for the student to be able to pass this course.

nodes.title: Fund Cncpts Elm School Math
nodes.description: Topics include geometry, measurement, probability, statistics, algebra, number theory, and problem solving to provide background for future instruction in elementary and middle school mathematics. Prerequisite: Three years of secondary school math.
nodes.other_requirements: Three years of secondary school math. ECP, ECSP, EDML and ELK6 majors only; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: College Algebra
nodes.description: Sets, relations, functions with particular attention to properties of algebraic, exponential, logarithmic functions, their graphs and applications in preparation for MATH 1212. May not be taken for credit concurrently with, or following receipt of, credit for any mathematics course numbered MATH 1212 or above. Pre/co-requisites: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry.

nodes.title: Elementary School Math
nodes.description: Operations with real numbers: decimals, fractions, percents, integers. Set operations, Venn diagrams, algebra, and problem solving provide background for future instruction in elementary/middle school mathematics. Prerequisite: Three years of secondary school math.
nodes.other_requirements: Three years of secondary school math. ECP, ECSP, EDML and ELK6 majors only. Instructor permission may be required. Contact krisan.geary@uvm.edu for more information.

nodes.title: Adv Ordinary Diff Equations
nodes.description: Linear and nonlinear systems, approximate solutions, existence, uniqueness, dependence on initial conditions, stability, asymptotic behavior, singularities, self-adjoint problems. Prerequisite: Graduate student or Instructor permission; knowledge of differential equations required.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student or Instructor permission; knowledge of differential equations required. Open to Degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to limits and differential/integral calculus with a wide variety of applications. Students interested in intensive use of mathematics should take MATH 1234. Credit not awarded for both MATH 1212 and MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248. See MATH 1242. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system: MATH 1012 or MATH 1034 or ALEKS MPA of at least 61; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Basic Combinatorial Theory
nodes.description: Introduction to basic combinatorial principles emphasizing problem-solving techniques. Enumeration, generating functions, Fibonacci numbers, pigeonhole principle, inclusion-exclusion, and graph theory. Prerequisites: MATH 2055 (preferred) or CS 1640.
nodes.other_requirements: MATH 2055 (preferred). Prereqs enforced by the system. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: IS:Functional Analysis & PDE
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Adv Engineering Mathematics
nodes.description: Differential equations, Laplace transforms, and systems of differential equations; brief introduction to Fourier series. Examples from engineering and physical sciences. Credit not granted for both MATH 3230 and MATH 3201. No credit for Mathematics majors. Prerequisite: MATH 2248. Co-requisites: Preferred: MATH 2522 or MATH 2544; or MATH 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not granted for both MATH 3230 and MATH 3201. No credit for Mathematics majors. Co-requisites: Preferred: MATH 2522 or MATH 2544; or MATH 2500. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research for the Doctoral Dissertation.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Smooth Manifolds
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Two semesters of real analysis, and other experience with point-set topology; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Mathematics
nodes.description: Emphasizing proofs, fundamental mathematical concepts and techniques are investigated within the context of number theory and other topics. Prerequisite: MATH 1234 or MATH 1242. Credit not given for more than one of MATH 2055 and CS 1640.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not given for more than one of MATH 2055 and CS 1640. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Theory of Func of Complex Var
nodes.description: Complex functions, differentiation and the Cauchy-Riemann equations, power and Laurent series, integration, calculus of residues, contour integration, isolated singularities, conformal mapping, harmonic functions. Prerequisite: Two semesters of real analysis required.
nodes.other_requirements: Two semesters of real analysis required. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Exploring Modern Mathematics
nodes.description: Introduction to mathematics of finite systems with applications, such as probability, statistics, graph theory, fair division and apportionment problems, voting systems. Prerequisites: Two years of secondary school algebra, MATH 1012, or MATH 1034.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of secondary school algebra. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis.

nodes.title: Fndmntls of Financial Math
nodes.description: Students will be introduced to the basic ideas and algebraic structures of interest theory, time-value of money, annuities, loans, bonds, cash-flows and portfolios. Prerequisites: MATH 1224, MATH 1248 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students; Asynchronous online

nodes.title: Abstract Algebra III
nodes.description: Advanced group theory and field theory. Prerequisite: Two semesters of abstract algebra required.
nodes.other_requirements: Two semesters of abstract algebra required.

nodes.title: Eng Math Linear Algebra Lab
nodes.description: Survey of the fundamental concepts of linear algebra necessary to describe the solution space of a linear differential equation and for solving systems of linear differential equations. Credit not awarded following receipt of credit for MATH 2522 or MATH 2544. Prerequisite: MATH 1234. Co-requisites: MATH 1248 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded following receipt of credit for MATH 2522 or MATH 2544. Co-requisites: MATH 1248 or MATH 1242. Prereqs enforced by the system:MATH 1234; Coreq:MATH 1248or1242; Open to Degree and PACE students; Asynchronous online

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Graduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Linear Algebra
nodes.description: Vector spaces, linear independence, bases, linear transformations, matrices, determinants, change of basis characteristic equations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, with applications. Emphasis on understanding and gaining facility with these concepts including proofs. Credit not given for both MATH 2522 and MATH 2544. Prerequisite: MATH 1248 or MATH 1242. Co-requisite: MATH 2248 or MATH 2055.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: MATH 2248 or MATH 2055. Credit not given for both MATH 2522 and MATH 2544. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to calculus of functions of one variable including: limits, continuity, techniques and applications of differentiation and integration. Credit not given for more than one course in the pair MATH 1212, MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248 or MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 1248 or above. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system.

nodes.title: Calculus II
nodes.description: Vectors and vector operations. Techniques and applications of integration. Polar coordinates, Taylor polynomials, sequences and series, power series. Credit will not be given for both MATH 1248 and MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 2248 or above. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1234. C- or better in MATH 021.

nodes.title: Applied Linear Algebra
nodes.description: Vectors, matrices, linear independence, vector spaces (with focus on real n-space), determinants, linear transformations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Applications from engineering and the sciences incorporated through required computer assignments. Credit not given for both MATH 2522 and MATH 2544. Prerequisite: MATH 1248 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not given for both MATH 2522 and MATH 2544. Open to Degree and PACE students. System waitlisting enabled for this section. This is a lecture-based course. Topics will be presented with a level of depth and rigor appropriate for students pursuing degrees in technical fields such as engineering, computer science, or the physical sciences. Math Majors should take Math 2544 Linear Algebra. Materials: WebAssign access for Linear Algebra with Applications, 9th edition, by Garreth Williams ISBN 1284154696 . WebAssign includes access to an e-text, as well as homework exercises. Matlab Assignments: No prior experience with Matlab is assumed. Matlab is used throughout the course and is available on the UVM CEMS computers and it can be downloaded from:https://www.uvm.edu/cems/installing_matlab_students

nodes.title: IS in Multi-Calculus
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Junior/Senior standing; approval of Department Chair.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior/Senior standing; approval of Department Chair. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Thry Functions Real Variables
nodes.description: Lebesgue measure and integration theory, Monotone and Dominated Convergence Theorems and applications, product measures, basic theory of LP-spaces. Prerequisite: Two semesters of real analysis required.
nodes.other_requirements: Two semesters of real analysis required. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Principles of Complex Systms 2
nodes.description: Detailed exploration of distribution, transportation, small-world, scale-free, social, biological, organizational networks; generative mechanisms; measurement and statistics of network properties; network dynamics; contagion processes. Students from all disciplines welcomed. Pre/co-requisites: MATH 6701, CSYS 6701, calculus, and statistics required. Cross-listed with: CSYS 6713.
nodes.other_requirements: calculus, and statistics required

nodes.title: Elementary Number Theory
nodes.description: Divisibility, prime numbers, Diophantine equations, congruence of numbers, and methods of solving congruences. A significant portion of the course devoted to individual and/or team projects. Prerequisites: MATH 2055 or CS 1640; MATH 2248, MATH 2522, or MATH 2544.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Math of Quantum Computing
nodes.description: For advanced students in the indicated fields. Lectures, reports, and directed readings on advanced topics. Credit as arranged. Offered as occasion warrants.
nodes.other_requirements: For advanced students in the indicated fields. Lectures, reports, and directed readings on advanced topics. Credit as arranged. Offered as occasion warrants. Students should know linear algebra, and a little bit of trigonometry and modular arithmetic ("numbers modulo n").

nodes.title: Transitional Calculus
nodes.description: Intended to make the transition from MATH 1212 to MATH 2248. Topics are similar to MATH 1248 but recognizing different backgrounds of students in MATH 1212 versus MATH 1234. Credit will not be given for both MATH 1248 and MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 2248 or above. Prerequisite: B or better in MATH 1212, or B or better in MATH 1224.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Gr Chaos,Fractals&Dynmcl Systm
nodes.description: Discrete and continuous dynamical systems, Julia sets, the Mandelbrot set, period doubling, renormalization, Henon map, phase plane analysis and Lorenz equations. Credit not awarded for both MATH 5766 and MATH 3766. Prerequisites: Graduate student or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: CSYS 5766.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student or Instructor permission. The course requires some experience with Linear Algebra, and programing experience is suggested (but not required).

nodes.title: Pre-Calculus Mathematics
nodes.description: Skills in working with numerical, algebraic, and trigonometric expressions are developed in preparation for MATH 1234. May not be taken for credit concurrently with, or following receipt of, credit for any mathematics course numbered MATH 1234 or above. Prerequisite: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Abstract Algebra I
nodes.description: Basic theory of groups, rings, fields, homomorphisms, and isomorphisms. Prerequisites: MATH 2468 or MATH 2551 or C- or better in MATH 2055; MATH 2522 or MATH 2544.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 2055; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: IS:Numerical Diff Equations
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Anyl in Several Real Vars I
nodes.description: Properties of the real numbers, basic topology of metric spaces, infinite sequences and series, continuity. Prerequisites: MATH 2468 or MATH 2551 or C- or better in MATH 2055; MATH 2248; MATH 2522 or MATH 2544.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 2055

nodes.title: Topics in Combinatorics
nodes.description: Topics will vary each semester and may include combinatorial designs, coding theory, topological graph theory, cryptography. Course is repeatable for credit. Prerequisite: MATH 3551 or MATH 5678.

nodes.title: Principles of Complex Systms 1
nodes.description: Introduction to fundamental concepts of complex systems. Topics include: emergence, scaling phenomena and mechanisms, multi-scale systems, failure, robustness, collective social phenomena, complex networks. Students from all disciplines welcomed. Pre/co-requisites: Calculus, statistics required; linear algebra, differential equations, computer programming recommended. Cross-listed with: MATH 6701.
nodes.other_requirements: linear algebra, differential equations, computer programming recommended

nodes.title: Development of Mathematics
nodes.description: Project-based course. Historical development of mathematical sciences emphasizing interrelations among them. Individual assignments correspond to background and interests of students. Prerequisite: Nine hours of college mathematics.
nodes.other_requirements: Nine hours of college mathematics

nodes.title: Geometry for Educators
nodes.description: An examination of geometric relationships using reasoning and proof. Topics include Euclidean, non-Euclidean and finite geometries, affine transformations, constructions, and spatial geometry. Provides background for future instruction in middle and high school geometry. Credit not awarded for students majoring or minoring in Mathematics. Prerequisites: Three credits of Mathematics at MATH 1111 or above, minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Three credits of Mathematics at MATH 1111 or above, minimum Sophomore standing. ECP, ECSP, EDML, SE and ELK6 majors only; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Groups and Rings
nodes.description: An introduction to the basic concepts of abstract algebra emphasizing examples, including modular arithmetic, symmetric groups, cyclic groups, polynomial rings, homomorphisms, and isomorphisms. May not be taken concurrently with or after MATH 3551. Prerequisite: MATH 2055 (preferred) or CS 1640.
nodes.other_requirements: May not be taken concurrently with or after MATH 3551. MATH 2055 (preferred).

nodes.title: Math Models & Their Analysis
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus II
nodes.description: Techniques and applications of integration. An introduction to multi-variable calculus: partial derivatives and double integrals. Prerequisite: MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Calculus III
nodes.description: Vector-valued functions. Calculus of functions of several variables: partial derivatives, gradient, divergence, curl, multiple integrals, line integrals, Stokes' and Green's theorems. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1248 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1248 or MATH 1242

nodes.title: Algebra for Educators
nodes.description: Algebraic concepts and relationships are explored and developed. Linear, quadratic, and exponential functions are featured. Credit not awarded for students majoring or minoring in Mathematics. Prerequisite: 3 credits of Math numbered MATH 1111 or above.
nodes.other_requirements: 3 credits of Math numbered MATH 1111 or above; ECP, ECSP, EDML and ELK6 majors only; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Principles of Complex Systms 1
nodes.description: Introduction to fundamental concepts of complex systems. Topics include: emergence, scaling phenomena, and mechanisms, multi-scale systems, failure, robustness, collective social phenomena, complex networks. Students from all disciplines welcomed. Pre/co-requisites: Calculus and statistics required; linear algebra, differential equations, and computer programming recommended but not required. Cross-listed with: CSYS 6701.
nodes.other_requirements: linear algebra, differential equations, and computer programming recommended but not required.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Honors Thesis
nodes.description: Program of reading and research culminating in written thesis and oral presentation. Honors notation appears on transcript and Commencement Program. Contact department chairperson for procedures.
